answer.content:
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>Interpreter</title>
    <style>
        body {
            font-family: monospace;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>

<script>
    // Define a function for the interpreter
    function interpreter(code) {
        let output = "";
        let stack = [];

        // Define a dictionary to map keywords to their meanings
        let keywords = {
            "if": "conditional",
            "else": "alternative",
            "for": "loop",
            "while": "loop",
            "print": "output"
        };

        // Split the code into lines
        let lines = code.split("\n");

        // Iterate over each line
        for (let line of lines) {
            // Remove leading and trailing whitespace
            line = line.trim();

            // Check if the line is a keyword
            if (line in keywords) {
                // If it's a keyword, execute the corresponding action
                if (line === "if") {
                    if (stack.length > 0 && stack[0] === keywords["if"]) {
                        stack.shift();
                        output += "Executing if statement\n";
                    } else {
                        output += "Invalid if statement\n";
                    }
                } else if (line === "else") {
                    if (stack.length > 0 && stack[0] === keywords["else"]) {
                        stack.shift();
                        output += "Executing else statement\n";
                    } else {
                        output += "Invalid else statement\n";
                    }
                } else if (line === "for") {
                    if (stack.length > 0 && stack[0] === keywords["for"]) {
                        stack.shift();
                        output += "Executing for loop\n";
                    } else {
                        output += "Invalid for loop\n";
                    }
                } else if (line === "while") {
                    if (stack.length > 0 && stack[0] === keywords["while"]) {
                        stack.shift();
                        output += "Executing while loop\n";
                    } else {
                        output += "Invalid while loop\n";
                    }
                } else if (line === "print") {
                    if (stack.length > 0 && stack[0] === keywords["print"]) {
                        stack.shift();
                        output += "Executing print statement\n";
                    } else {
                        output += "Invalid print statement\n";
                    }
                }
            } else {
                // If it's not a keyword, add it to the output
                output += line + "\n";
            }
        }

        return output;
    }

    // Get the input code from the user
    let code = prompt("Enter your code: ");
    let output = interpreter(code);

    // Print the output
    console.log(output);
</script>
</body>
</html>

messages.content: Generate a single HTML file with JavaScript demonstrating the concept Interpreter. Only respond in a single HTML file.
